extraction.one_line_summary: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren won the 15km technical ITT in Sainte-Memmie by 12 seconds over SCHACHMANN Maximilian, taking time on all GC rivals.
tactical_patterns: KRAGH ANDERSEN negative-split pacing strategy paid dividends: 3s behind ASGREEN at checkpoint, 12s ahead at finish after superior second half.
BILBAO Pello reversed typical climber pattern: flew first half (3s up on DE GENDT Thomas) but faded second half, revealing punchy power without sustained TT endurance.
SCHACHMANN Maximilian posted best intermediate by 7s yet lost to KRAGH ANDERSEN by 12s, suggesting overpacing on technical early circuit sections costs more than raw power gains.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian home-road advantage failed to translate on technical 15km circuit despite aggressive riding style past dropped riders, finishing 24s down in 11th.

rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: First professional ITT win with perfect negative split execution reveals hidden GC threat potential on technical parcours
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: Extended GC lead to 58s over KRAGH ANDERSEN despite stage loss, proved superior to pure climbers on rolling TT
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Third place confirms consistency in solo efforts after Brussels-Kuurne win, reliable domestique-plus option for hilly stage races
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: Fifth place strong for pure climber but second-half fade suggests cannot sustain power on flatter technical TT sections
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Home-circuit advantage and flamboyant riding style failed to deliver repeat of 2019 TdF form, 24s deficit reveals current ITT limitations
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: New equipment failed to compensate for poor TT legs, 50s loss confirms continued weakness in discipline
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: New team, new bike, but 53s loss shows age-related ITT decline becoming significant GC liability
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: neutral: 46s loss acceptable for young climber stepping up to World Tour, avoided catastrophic time hemorrhage in second half
